info.title: Receipt Assistant API
info.description: Double-entry ledger backend. The v1 resources (transactions, postings, accounts, documents) are under active construction — see issues #33 (schema), #35 (transactions API), #36 (accounts API).

opening_balance_minor.description: Signed integer in the currency's minor unit (cents for USD, 1 for JPY, satoshi for BTC). Never store money as float.

get.summary: Health check
Build metadata
List accounts (tree by default, flat with ?flat=true)

200.description: Account list (flat: array of Account; default: array of AccountTreeNode roots)

get.summary: Get one account
Account balance as of a date
Register (checkbook view) with running balance
List transactions
Get a transaction

delete.description: Default: only draft/error transactions may be hard-deleted; posted/voided return 409 (must-void-instead). ?hard=true forces a hard delete of any non-reconciled transaction (postings + document_links cascade). Reconciled transactions always reject — unreconcile first.

schema.description: Force hard delete of posted/voided transactions. Reconciled is still rejected.

409.description: Must void instead (posted, no ?hard=true), or transaction is reconciled.

post.description: Pure state flip: `reconciled` → `posted`, with an audit event. Match-side state (e.g. bank-line associations) is intentionally NOT cleaned up here — callers compose this with their own match cleanup. Used as the escape hatch before `DELETE /v1/transactions/:id?hard=true` or `DELETE /v1/documents/:id?cascade=true&hard=true` on a reconciled row.

409.description: Transaction is not in reconciled state

post.summary: Add a posting to a transaction

get.summary: List postings
Get a posting

200.description: Duplicate upload detected — returns the existing document row

get.summary: Get document metadata

schema.description: Include soft-deleted documents in the response. Default false.

delete.summary: Delete a document. Soft-deletes by default; ?hard=true removes the row and quarantines the file under .trash/; ?cascade=true also handles linked transactions.
delete.description: Default: soft delete (sets deleted_at). ?hard=true: hard delete (row removed; file moved to <uploads_dir>/.trash/<timestamp>__<basename>, NOT unlinked); requires no remaining links unless ?cascade=true is also set. ?cascade=true: linked posted transactions are voided, draft/error transactions are hard-deleted, voided transactions are left intact, reconciled transactions abort the operation with 409. ?cascade=true&hard=true: every linked transaction is hard-deleted (postings cascade), the document row is removed, and the image file is moved to .trash/ (never unlinked). Reconciled transactions always block hard cascades — unreconcile first.

schema.description: Hard-delete the row and quarantine the on-disk image into <uploads_dir>/.trash/. The image is moved, not unlinked — recoverable by hand. Default false.
Also process transactions linked to this document. Combine with hard=true for full purge.

409.description: Hard delete refused because document has links (use cascade), or cascade refused because a linked transaction is reconciled.

get.summary: Stream document binary content

post.summary: Link a document to a transaction (idempotent)

delete.summary: Unlink a document from a transaction

404.description: Document not found or not deleted

post.summary: Upload N files for agent classification + extraction. Returns 202 with per-file ingest ids.

get.summary: List ingestion batches (most recent first)
Get one batch with all child ingests

Server-Sent Events stream of per-ingest + per-batch + reconcile events. Connects, sends a `hello` catch-up frame with current counts + status, then relays `job.*`, `batch.*`, and `reconcile.*` events as they fire. Closes on terminal status (reconciled / failed) or client disconnect.
List ingests across batches
Get one ingest with produced reverse-lookup

post.summary: Run the reconcile pipeline over an extracted batch. Idempotent: a second call on a reconciled batch returns the stored result.

202.description: Batch is already mid-reconcile on another caller; response carries a `poll` URL to fetch the final result.

409.description: Batch is not in a reconcilable state (still extracting, or entered reconcile_error).

get.summary: Fetch the latest reconcile result for a batch.

post.summary: Apply one or more reconcile proposals. For dedup proposals this voids the duplicate via the ledger service.
Reject one or more reconcile proposals. Marks proposals as rejected with no ledger mutation.

get.summary: Spend aggregated by category / account / payee
Time-series trend (MoM / YoY) of expense spend
Assets - liabilities at a point in time
Inflows vs outflows over a range, bucketed by month
Merchant detail + KPIs
get.description: `id` accepts either the merchant row's UUID or its kebab-case `brand_id`.

get.summary: Transactions for a merchant, newest first, keyset-paginated
Get a place by id (full multilingual record).
get.description: Returns the place row with all multilingual columns plus refs to any cached photos. Photo bytes are streamed separately via /v1/places/{id}/photos/{rank}/content.

patch.description: Currently exposes only `custom_name_zh` — the user override that wins over `display_name_zh` in the UI fallback chain. Pass null to clear.

get.summary: Stream the binary of a cached Google Places photo.
get.description: 0-based rank within the place's photos[] (as returned by the v1 Places API at first fetch). 404 if the rank is out of range or the local file copy is missing.
